title: NoSQL Indexing Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about indexing and performance optimization in NoSQL databases.

questions.question: What is the primary role of an 'Index' in a NoSQL database?
options: To encrypt all documents.
To allow the database to jump directly to relevant records instead of performing a slow collection scan.
To automatically generate a backup of the database.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: What happens if you perform a query on a field that is NOT indexed?
options: The database crashes.
The database performs a 'Collection Scan' (reading every single document in the collection), which is incredibly slow for large datasets.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
None of the above.

questions.question: Which type of NoSQL index can automatically delete documents from a collection after a certain period of time?
options: Single Field
Compound
TTL (Time To Live)
Sparse

questions.question: What's the meaning of a 'Compound Index' in NoSQL?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
An index that combines multiple fields together (e.g., user_id and status) to provide better performance for complex queries.
A type of database indexing only for numbers.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is a 'Sparse Index' and why is it useful for saving resources?
options: An index that only includes documents that actually contain the indexed field, saving storage and memory for sparse datasets.
An index that is only used for small databases.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To improve the user experience.
